perturbation.original_text: "Project" shall mean any undertaking involving the provision of nuclear consulting or strategic advisory services to any entity in the United Arab Emirates.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Project' is significantly broadened. Originally, it was specifically tied to nuclear consulting and strategic advisory services in the United Arab Emirates. By removing that specific language, any undertaking, even those outside the scope of the original agreement, could be construed as a 'Project'. This creates an internal contradiction as Article II outlines the general scope of the agreement as being within the nuclear consulting/advisory space, and this change effectively removes that limit. This is directly contradictory because section 2.1 uses the term project, while the definition of project has been altered to make it more generic and not related to the nuclear consulting field.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.1 General Scope. During the term of this Agreement, the Parties shall actively seek attractive Projects and, in pursuit of any potential Project, shall promote the Lightbridge-Lloyds strategic alliance whenever given the opportunity to do so. Each Party shall present to the other Party every potential Project which such Party desires to pursue.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Project' was specific to nuclear consulting and advisory services in the UAE. The changed definition broadens the meaning of 'Project' to 'any undertaking'. This contradicts Section 2.1, which states that 'the Parties shall actively seek attractive Projects and, in pursuit of any potential Project, shall promote the Lightbridge-Lloyds strategic alliance whenever given the opportunity to do so'. The change removes the implicit limitation of 'Project' being related to the nuclear consulting/advisory scope, making the requirement to promote the strategic alliance for *any* undertaking contradictory to the original intent and scope of the agreement. It now includes undertakings outside of nuclear consulting.

perturbation.original_text: Section 2.2 Relationship of Parties. With respect to any Project, regardless of which Party identified such Project, the Parties agree that Lloyds shall act as prime contractor on any Project and that Lightbridge shall have a right of first refusal to participate in such Project as a sub- contractor to Lloyds, and shall be entitled to perform such work which would generate up to twenty-five percent (25%) of the total fees payable to Lloyds as general contractor under the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2.2 Relationship of Parties. With respect to any Project, regardless of which Party identified such Project, the Parties agree that Lloyds shall act as prime contractor on any Project.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the clause granting Lightbridge a 'right of first refusal' on Projects directly contradicts the background section which states that the parties will be 'cooperatively combining their considerable experience, expertise and financial and market resources, they can increase the volume of business they can conduct'. Removing Lightbridge's right of first refusal undermines the intent of a cooperative alliance, creating an in-text contradiction in relation to the background, where it establishes that both parties will be cooperatively combining their resources to increase the amount of business they conduct.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BACKGROUND Lightbridge and Lloyds believe that, by cooperatively combining their considerable experience, expertise and financial and market resources, they can increase the volume of business they can conduct in in the area of nuclear consulting and strategic advisory services in the United Arab Emirates.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Lightbridge a 'right of first refusal' on projects, ensuring their participation and aligning with the background's intention of cooperative resource combination. The changed text removes this right, directly contradicting the background statement that both parties will 'cooperatively combin[e] their considerable experience, expertise and financial and market resources, [to] increase the volume of business they can conduct.' This removal undermines the cooperative alliance and creates an in-text contradiction because the background section establishes that the parties will work together to increase business volume, which the removal of the right of first refusal directly hinders.

perturbation.original_text: Section 5.2 Remedies. Upon the occurrence and during the continuation of any Event of Default, the non-Defaulting Party may immediately terminate this Agreement by giving notice to the Defaulting Party. The non-Defaulting Party's remedy set forth in the immediately preceding sentence is not intended to be exclusive and is in addition to, and may be exercised concurrently with, any other right or remedy that may otherwise be available to the Non-Defaulting Party at law or in equity. Without limiting the foregoing and for the avoidance of doubt, the Non-Defaulting Party may recover damages against the Defaulting Party even if this Agreement is terminated (including damages arising out of such termination).
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5.2 Remedies. Upon the occurrence and during the continuation of any Event of Default, the non-Defaulting Party may immediately terminate this Agreement by giving notice to the Defaulting Party. The non-Defaulting Party's remedy set forth in the immediately preceding sentence is not intended to be exclusive and is in addition to, and may be exercised concurrently with, any other right or remedy that may otherwise be available to the Non-Defaulting Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence 'Without limiting the foregoing and for the avoidance of doubt, the Non-Defaulting Party may recover damages against the Defaulting Party even if this Agreement is terminated (including damages arising out of such termination).' creates an in-text contradiction. The prior sentence states that remedies are 'in addition to' any other right, creating an expectation that damage recovery is among those rights. Removing the explicit right to damages introduces ambiguity as to whether such recovery is still permissible upon termination, potentially contradicting the stated intention of additional remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5.2 Remedies. Upon the occurrence and during the continuation of any Event of Default, the non-Defaulting Party may immediately terminate this Agreement by giving notice to the Defaulting Party. The non-Defaulting Party's remedy set forth in the immediately preceding sentence is not intended to be exclusive and is in addition to, and may be exercised concurrently with, any other right or remedy that may otherwise be available to the Non-Defaulting Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the explicit right to recover damages against the defaulting party, even if the agreement is terminated, directly contradicts the stated intention of additional remedies in the contradicted text, as it creates ambiguity regarding whether such recovery is still permissible upon termination, breaking the initial expectation of additional rights and remedies, as stated in Section 5.2.
